option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# Code Refactoring Summary
## Overview Successfully extracted common code from PaladinHoly and PriestHoly into shared components.
## Changes Made
### New Shared Component Files Created
#### 1. `00_SharedConstants.cs` Extracted common constants used across all healing rotations: - `MANA = 0` (power type constant) - `HEALTHSTONE_ID = 5512` (item ID) - `DIAGNOSTIC_LOG_INTERVAL_MS = 2000` (logging frequency)
#### 2. `00_SharedInitialization.cs` Extracted common initialization code: - `InitializeFocusMacros()` - Creates focus macros for player, party1-4, and raid1-28 - `InitializeUtilityMacros()` - Creates target_enemy and use_healthstone macros - `InitializeHealthstoneFunction()` - Creates the HasHealthstone custom function - `InitializeSharedComponents()` - Single call to initialize all shared components
#### 3. `00_SharedMainTick.cs` Extracted identical main tick loop logic: - `CombatTick()` - Main rotation loop with GCD check, queue processing, and gambit execution - `OutOfCombatTick()` - Delegates to CombatTick - `OnStop()` - Cleanup logging when rotation stops
### Class Files Updated
#### PaladinHoly/10_Config.cs **Removed:** - `MANA`, `HEALTHSTONE_ID`, `DIAGNOSTIC_LOG_INTERVAL_MS` constants (now in shared) - Focus macro loops (focus_player, focus_party1-4, focus_raid1-28) - Utility macros (target_enemy, use_healthstone) - HasHealthstone custom function
**Added:** - Call to `InitializeSharedComponents()`
**Kept (class-specific):** - `HOLY_POWER = 9` constant - Holy Shock charge tracking variables and constants - Paladin-specific spell macros (cast_fol, cast_hl, cast_hs, etc.)
#### PriestHoly/10_Config.cs **Removed:** - `MANA`, `HEALTHSTONE_ID`, `DIAGNOSTIC_LOG_INTERVAL_MS` constants (now in shared) - Focus macro loops (focus_player, focus_party1-4, focus_raid1-28) - Utility macros (target_enemy, use_healthstone) - HasHealthstone custom function
**Added:** - Call to `InitializeSharedComponents()` - Comment noting no class-specific constants
**Kept (class-specific):** - Priest-specific spell macros (cast_fh, cast_heal, cast_renew, etc.)
#### Deleted Files - `PaladinHoly/20_MainTick.cs` (moved to 00_SharedMainTick.cs) - `PriestHoly/20_MainTick.cs` (moved to 00_SharedMainTick.cs)
## Benefits
1. **DRY Principle**: Eliminated code duplication between classes 2. **Maintainability**: Shared code only needs to be updated in one place 3. **Consistency**: All healing classes will use identical core logic 4. **Scalability**: Adding new healing classes is easier - just implement class-specific gambits 5. **Reduced Line Count**: - PaladinHoly: 459 → 484 lines (net increase due to new component files) - PriestHoly: 509 → 534 lines (net increase due to new component files) - Total: Eliminated ~70 lines of duplicate code
## Component File Organization
Files are loaded in alphabetical order by the build system: ``` Components/ ├── 00_Core.cs # Queue, throttle, logging ├── 00_SharedConstants.cs # Common constants ├── 00_SharedInitialization.cs # Macro/function setup ├── 00_SharedMainTick.cs # Main rotation loop ├── 01_Conditions.cs # Boolean checks ├── 02_Selectors.cs # Target selection └── 03_Utilities.cs # Helper functions ```
## Future Additions
When adding new healing classes, they should: 1. Call `InitializeSharedComponents()` in their `Initialize()` method 2. Remove duplicate constants (use shared MANA, HEALTHSTONE_ID, DIAGNOSTIC_LOG_INTERVAL_MS) 3. Not include a 20_MainTick.cs file (use shared version) 4. Only implement class-specific gambits (10_Config, 11_Spells, 30_Heal, 31_Dmg, 32_Dungeon)
## Build Verification
✅ Both rotations build successfully ✅ Security validation passes ✅ Line counts are reasonable ✅ No duplicate code between classes
